text.title: langchain-observability Failure Modes
text.summary: Four high-impact observability failures: silent untraced runs, PII export, swallowed callback exceptions, sampling that hides the bug.

antipattern.symptom: An incident report says "agent returned garbage at 03:00 UTC" and no trace exists.
antipattern.root-cause: LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2 was off in the prod env, or LANGCHAIN_API_KEY was missing — silently disabling LangSmith.
antipattern.fix: Assert LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2==true at app startup; refuse to boot in production without it.
antipattern.detector: Hit /healthz; verify it logs "tracing=enabled"; if missing, fail the deploy.

antipattern.symptom: Customer SSN appears in a LangSmith trace, exposed to anyone with project access.
antipattern.root-cause: redact_payloads was false and pii_posture was not declared.
antipattern.fix: Always set a redaction callback in front of the LangSmith handler; never trust upstream sanitisation alone.
antipattern.detector: Run a canary string ("SSN-CANARY-1234") through the chain; grep the trace UI for it.

antipattern.symptom: Custom callback throws but the chain keeps going and you never know.
antipattern.root-cause: BaseCallbackHandler swallows exceptions by default to avoid breaking chains.
antipattern.fix: Override on_chain_error to push to a dead-letter queue; or wrap callback in try/except + structured log.
antipattern.detector: Inject a raise in the callback; verify alerts fire.

antipattern.symptom: Sample rate of 0.05 means 95% of failed runs have no trace.
antipattern.root-cause: Sampling was applied uniformly instead of head-based-on-error.
antipattern.fix: Either sample at 1.0 in production for the first 30 days, or use tail-based sampling that keeps every run with status=error.
antipattern.detector: Compare error count in logs vs traced error count in LangSmith; ratio should be ≥0.95.
